spring-boot-2-jdbc-with-h2/pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade the Spring Boot parent version to 3.3.13 to align with the Java 21 migration and access newer framework features.
spring-boot-2-jdbc-with-h2/pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3.0+ requires Java 17 minimum, and specific 3.3.x features/fixes are targeted; while not strictly a compile error for just the version bump itself in isolation, it is part of the breaking migration path to modernize the stack.

spring-boot-2-jdbc-with-h2/pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property to 21 to target the new JDK version.
spring-boot-2-jdbc-with-h2/pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project would still compile and run on Java 17, failing to utilize Java 21 features or enforce the new baseline.

spring-boot-2-jdbc-with-h2/src/main/java.general_idea: Reorder arguments in jdbcTemplate.queryForObject to match Spring Boot 3 / Spring Framework 6 API signature.
spring-boot-2-jdbc-with-h2/src/main/java.why_exists: Compile error: The method signature for queryForObject changed in Spring Framework 6 (used by Spring Boot 3) to require the RowMapper before the varargs arguments, whereas Spring Framework 5 allowed the varargs before the RowMapper.
spring-boot-2-jdbc-with-h2/src/main/java.before:
return jdbcTemplate.queryForObject("select * from student where id=?", new Object[]{id},
                new BeanPropertyRowMapper<Student>(Student.class));
spring-boot-2-jdbc-with-h2/src/main/java.after:
return jdbcTemplate.queryForObject("select * from student where id=?", new BeanPropertyRowMapper<Student>(Student.class),
                new Object[]{id});
